description: Retrieves a locator for a specific element on the page based on the provided parameters.

constraints.description: Optional rules influencing how the locator is selected, such as visibility, enablement, and allowed/disallowed strategies.

mustBeVisible.description: If true, the element must be visible on the page to qualify as a valid locator.

mustBeEnabled.description: If true, the element must not be disabled to qualify as a valid locator.

prefer.description: Preferred locator strategies, in priority order. The first matching strategy will be used.

forbid.description: Locator strategies to avoid. Helps prevent use of brittle or unstable locators.

driverSession.description: Unique identifier for the active browser session in which the locator search is performed.

intent.description: The user and agent intent describing the element to locate on the page, used to guide locator strategy selection.

agentIntent.description: The normalized intent produced by the agent for tool discovery, routing, or execution.

userIntent.description: The original intent as expressed by the user.

openaiApiKey.description: OpenAI authentication token for verifying and authorizing the locator retrieval request.

openaiUri.description: OpenAI API endpoint URI for the request.

token.description: G4 authentication token for verifying and authorizing the locator retrieval request.

name: g4.ResolveLocator

driverSession.description: The browser session identifier associated with this locator result.

value.description: Locator details, including primary and fallback strategies, along with human-readable context.

primaryLocator.description: Main locator used to find the element, optimized for reliability.

value.description: Locator string (e.g., CSS selector or XPath) used in element identification.

using.description: Locator strategy type, such as 'css', 'xpath', or 'id'.

fallbackLocator.description: Alternative locator to use if the primary locator fails. Useful for elements with multiple stable selectors.

value.description: Fallback locator string (e.g., CSS selector or XPath).

using.description: Strategy type used by the fallback locator.

description.description: Optional human-readable description of the element being located, for debugging and clarity.

title: Resolve Element Locator
